hero.title: 5 Days at the Top of the World.
hero.titleAccent: Extended Himalayan Immersion Above the Clouds.
hero.intro: The longer Nagarkot stay that lets you properly settle into the mountains. Two full Himalayan sunrises, time to walk the ridgeline trails, a day in medieval Bhaktapur and the great temples of Kathmandu.
hero.heroImageAlt: Boudhanath Stupa Kathmandu Nepal at dusk golden light

excludes: Nepal Visa on Arrival (USD30 for 15 days)
Entrance Fees to temples and sites

overview.heading: 5 Days Nepal Himalayan Villa: The Full Picture
body: Five days is the right amount of time to experience Nepal's Kathmandu Valley fully and still have space at Nagarkot to breathe. Two nights on the Nagarkot ridge gives you two chances at the sunrise — insurance against one cloudy morning — and time to walk the forest reserve trails at your own pace.

This package covers Bhaktapur, the most perfectly preserved medieval city in the Himalayan foothills, along with Kathmandu's four great UNESCO heritage complexes, with nothing rushed and nothing missed.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Two Sunrises Over the Himalaya
items.body: Two nights at Nagarkot means two chances at a perfect sunrise — a sensible insurance in a country where mountain mornings can be glorious or cloud-covered. When it is clear, nothing in travel comes close to what you see from this ridge.
items.imageAlt: Nagarkot sunrise Himalayan views Nepal morning

items.title: Bhaktapur — Nepal's Medieval Masterpiece
items.body: Bhaktapur Durbar Square is often described as the finest example of Newari medieval architecture in the world. Pottery squares, wood-carved temples, pagodas and the 55-Window Palace — all without the crowds of Kathmandu.
items.imageAlt: Bhaktapur Durbar Square Nepal medieval temples

items.title: Kathmandu's Four UNESCO Sites
items.body: A full day in the valley for Pashupatinath's sacred ghats, Boudhanath's watching eyes, Swayambhunath's hilltop stupas and Kathmandu Durbar Square's pagoda temples — the four that define the city.
items.imageAlt: Kathmandu Durbar Square Nepal UNESCO pagoda temple

days.title: Arrive Kathmandu
days.description: Arrive at Tribhuvan International Airport and transfer to your Kathmandu hotel. Evening at leisure in Thamel.

days.title: Kathmandu UNESCO Temples
days.description: Full day visiting Kathmandu's four great UNESCO World Heritage Sites with your private guide.

days.title: Bhaktapur & Drive to Nagarkot
days.description: Morning in Bhaktapur, then drive up to Nagarkot for two nights on the Himalayan ridge.

days.title: Nagarkot Sunrise & Ridge Walks
days.description: First Himalayan sunrise from Nagarkot, then a day of ridgeline walks and valley exploration at your own pace.
suggestedActivities: Ridgeline trail to the viewing tower

days.title: Final Sunrise & Depart Kathmandu
days.description: Second sunrise opportunity at Nagarkot, then drive back to Kathmandu for your airport transfer.

sections.heading: 5 Day Nepal Tour Package from Malaysia — Why Two Nights at Nagarkot?
sections.body:
Two nights at Nagarkot is the difference between rushing the mountain experience and genuinely having it. The first morning you arrive is for orientation. The second morning — ideally crisp and clear — is when the full 200-kilometre sweep of the Himalaya appears. Everest, Lhotse, Makalu in the east; the Annapurnas in the west; and the whole arc between them.

This 5-day Nepal package from Malaysia gives you that second morning without compromise, combined with the full Kathmandu Valley cultural circuit and medieval Bhaktapur.

sections.heading: The Bhaktapur Difference
sections.body:
Bhaktapur is what many travellers consider the highlight of Nepal — more so than Kathmandu itself. As a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the three ancient Malla kingdom capitals, Bhaktapur has preserved its medieval core in extraordinary condition. Narrow brick lanes, pagoda temples, carved wood windows and the great Nyatapola temple rising five storeys above the Taumadhi Square.

Included as a full stop on the way up to Nagarkot, Bhaktapur is the cultural anchor of this five-day itinerary.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Nepal?
items.answer: Malaysian passport holders obtain a Visa on Arrival at Kathmandu's Tribhuvan International Airport. A 15-day tourist visa costs USD 30, a 30-day visa costs USD 50. Bring passport photos and the fee in cash. Dhesu provides full guidance with your booking.

items.question: Why two nights at Nagarkot?
items.answer: Mountain mornings in Nepal can be clear or cloudy — it depends on the season and the weather. Two nights gives you two chances at the Himalayan sunrise and a full free day to walk the ridgeline trails at a leisurely pace, rather than the rushed one-night version.

items.question: Is the drive to Nagarkot comfortable?
items.answer: Yes. It is approximately 32 kilometres from Kathmandu and takes about 1.5 hours by private car on a paved road that climbs through the Kathmandu Valley and up the ridge. Your private vehicle handles all transfers throughout the package.

items.question: What is there to do at Nagarkot beyond the sunrise?
items.answer: Nagarkot has a network of ridgeline trails through the Nagarkot Forest Reserve — gentle, forested walks with valley views. The viewing tower offers the widest panorama. The village itself is peaceful and the local teashops are worth exploring.

items.question: Can I add Pokhara or extend this trip?
items.answer: Yes. We can add a Pokhara extension or build a fully custom Nepal itinerary around your interests and schedule. Our 6-day Best of Nepal package is a popular next step that adds Phewa Lake and Sarangkot sunrise.
